facts.statement: Joel designed and shipped a product-wide UX overhaul of Finderly (AI Vision Technologies' AI home-repair app): 379 commits across 303 files in one month, June–July 2026.
The central decision was rebuilding Finderly as media-first and state-aware rather than chat-first: the home leads with a persistent capture hero, recent projects, and common fixes.

Joel rebuilt the capture pipeline end to end: ordered multi-capture (up to six photos or one video), a warm camera between shots, one review surface, and a 'living blueprint' analysis state driven by the real upload lifecycle.

Joel implemented in the product's React Native / Expo / TypeScript codebase; the color system became a Material HCT-generated system with 57 semantic roles, with judgment calls recorded in version control.
Joel cut features he had already built when they did not earn their complexity: drag-to-reorder for review media was removed, and sound recording moved out of review into an AI-requested diagnostic.

The overhaul ran as an agentic workflow: a design audit document as source of truth, AI agents building at Joel's direction, with taste, cuts, and QA staying human. The month ended with the frontend suite green and the branch merged to main.

Honest-interface decisions shipped: progress bars hidden while backend step tracking was untrustworthy, real privacy controls replacing a misleading cookie banner, and web AI consent recorded as passive rather than falsely claimed as explicit.
Public outcome metrics for Finderly (usage, conversion, retention) are not part of the public case study.
limitations: Case study documents scope and decisions, not usage metrics.

facts.statement: Joel’s most recent major shipped work is Finderly’s large redesign and front-end update, merged in July 2026. Finderly remains an active, ongoing product, so that release represents the latest major milestone rather than a finished endpoint.
limitations: Ongoing means the product continues to evolve; it does not mean the July 2026 release is unfinished or unshipped.
